security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:43 uses crypto.createHash('md5') — trivially reversible
security_controls.verdict_reason: MD5 is broken for password storage; no salt; bcrypt/argon2 required

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:26-28 — RSA private key hardcoded as string literal
security_controls.verdict_reason: Hardcoded key enables offline JWT forgery for any user; key compromise is permanent

security_controls.evidence: server.ts — rate limiting only on /rest/user/reset-password and 2FA endpoints; /rest/user/login unprotected
security_controls.verdict_reason: Brute-force of login endpoint unrestricted

security_controls.evidence: routes/2fa.ts — TOTP available but optional
security_controls.verdict_reason: 2FA available but not enforced; totpSecret stored in plaintext in DB

security_controls.evidence: frontend/src/app/login/login.component.ts:101 — localStorage.setItem('token',...)
security_controls.verdict_reason: localStorage is XSS-accessible; HttpOnly cookie preferred

security_controls.evidence: routes/verify.ts:83 — express-jwt@0.1.3 vulnerable to alg:none bypass
security_controls.verdict_reason: express-jwt@0.1.3 does not enforce algorithm; alg:none bypass possible

security_controls.evidence: frontend/src/app/app.guard.ts — AdminGuard is client-side only; no server enforcement for admin routes
security_controls.verdict_reason: Admin panel accessible by bypassing client-side Angular guard

security_controls.evidence: server.ts:369 — app.put('/api/Products/:id'...) intentionally commented out
security_controls.verdict_reason: Any user can modify any product price/description unauthenticated

security_controls.evidence: server.ts — no csurf or CSRF token middleware found
security_controls.verdict_reason: All state-changing endpoints vulnerable to CSRF from any origin

security_controls.evidence: routes/search.ts:23, routes/login.ts:37 — raw string interpolation in Sequelize queries
security_controls.verdict_reason: Direct SQLi exploitable on login and search endpoints without authentication

security_controls.evidence: routes/showProductReviews.ts:35 — $where clause with unparameterized input
security_controls.verdict_reason: MongoDB-style operator injection and sleep-based DoS possible

security_controls.evidence: Multiple routes accept unsanitized input; b2bOrder.ts:22 evaluates user-supplied data
security_controls.verdict_reason: No centralized input validation; eval() on user input enables RCE

security_controls.evidence: administration.component.ts:60,78 — bypassSecurityTrustHtml() on user-controlled data
security_controls.verdict_reason: Angular XSS protection deliberately bypassed in admin panel and feedback display

security_controls.evidence: server.ts:181-182 — app.use(cors()) with no origin restriction
security_controls.verdict_reason: Wildcard CORS allows any origin to make credentialed requests

security_controls.evidence: server.ts — helmet.contentSecurityPolicy() not configured; profile page uses unsafe-eval
security_controls.verdict_reason: No CSP header; XSS can load external scripts freely

security_controls.evidence: lib/insecurity.ts:26-44 — RSA private key and HMAC secret hardcoded as string literals
security_controls.verdict_reason: Production-equivalent secrets embedded in source code permanently compromised

security_controls.evidence: models/user.ts — password field uses MD5, no encryption for card numbers or PII
security_controls.verdict_reason: Sensitive fields (card numbers, addresses, TOTP secrets) stored in plaintext

security_controls.domain: File Parser and Outbound Request Controls
security_controls.evidence: routes/fileUpload.ts:45 — libxmljs2.parseXml with noent:true enables external entity processing
security_controls.verdict_reason: XXE via uploaded XML files can read local files (/etc/passwd) and cause DoS

security_controls.domain: File Parser and Outbound Request Controls
security_controls.evidence: routes/fileUpload.ts:36-52 — path.resolve check insufficient; directory traversal possible
security_controls.verdict_reason: Crafted ZIP entries can write files outside the intended uploads directory

security_controls.domain: File Parser and Outbound Request Controls
security_controls.evidence: routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts — fetches arbitrary URLs from user-supplied input
security_controls.verdict_reason: SSRF allows scanning internal network and accessing metadata services

security_controls.evidence: .dependabot/config.yml — express-jwt@0.1.3, sanitize-html@1.4.2, unzipper@0.9.15 explicitly pinned as vulnerable
security_controls.verdict_reason: Three vulnerable dependencies intentionally pinned; supply chain risk known and accepted by design

security_controls.evidence: Dockerfile:1,23 — base images use tags not digest-pinned; distroless final image has no tag
security_controls.verdict_reason: Image substitution attack possible; CI may build with different image versions

security_controls.evidence: .github/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml, zap_scan.yml — CodeQL and ZAP scans active
security_controls.verdict_reason: CodeQL (security-extended) and weekly ZAP scan present; some GitHub Actions unpinned

security_controls.evidence: server.ts:270-282 — /ftp, /encryptionkeys, /support/logs all have directory listing enabled
security_controls.verdict_reason: Confidential documents, JWT keys, and access logs publicly accessible

security_controls.evidence: lib/startup/registerWebsocketEvents.ts — Socket.io with CORS origin: localhost:4200 only
security_controls.verdict_reason: CORS origin restriction on WebSocket; no authentication required to connect

security_controls.evidence: server.ts — rate limiting on password reset and 2FA only; login and API endpoints unprotected
security_controls.verdict_reason: Rate limiting coverage too narrow; most attack-relevant endpoints unprotected

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.assessment: Automated dependency updates not detected in the repository. Patch-management posture depends on this control being in place — the team is reactive rather than proactive without it.

security_controls.domain: Operations Runtime and Supply Chain Controls
security_controls.assessment: Lockfile hygiene not detected in the repository. Patch-management posture depends on this control being in place — the team is reactive rather than proactive without it.
